verbatim_text: So how do language models do state tracking? So we begin by training a language model to output the final state given a sequence of inputs and after training we find that they're able to do this pretty well. So how are they doing this? I'm going to start with some hypotheses about what transformers can theoretically implement just to build some intuition. And later we can find evidence supporting or rejecting these hypotheses. So to start, one thing that transformers can do is this sequential algorithm. So first you can compute S1 in the first layer from I0 and I1. Next you can compute S1 from S1 and I2.
